text: The patient is positioned supine with the right side of the chest elevated at thirty degrees. Extended defibrillator pads are visible on the chest. Surgical manipulation is performed through three small trocars, each less than 1.2 centimeters, under a three-dimensional video screen.

A two-centimeter transverse incision is made in the right groin, where vasocannulas are established for indirect arterial cannulation using a darkened graft. The right common femoral artery is accessed, and cannulation is performed via the right femoral vein and right internal occipital vein using the Senegal technique.

Three trochars are placed in the right chest of the patient: one HMM trochar in the fifth intercostal space at the mid-axillary line for the 3D camera, one 12 mm trochar between the anterior axillary line and mid-clavicular line in the fifth intercostal space for the main surgical instrument, and one 5 mm trochar in the fourth anterior line for the second instrument.

The surgeon initiates CPP and inflows CO₂ into the chest cavity. Following lung deflation, the pericardium is opened two centimeters anterior to the frame knob, and a junction suture is placed on the pericardium.

The surgeon snares the superior vena cava using a pallium suture thread side tool, while the inferior vena cava is left free. The patient is positioned for surgery, and a rhiotomy is performed with four 0-pulling actions and junction sutures to expose the tumor.

The surgeon explores a gelatinous, reddish-brown, sphere-shaped mass with a smooth surface, approximately five centimeters in dimension. The mass is attached to the fossa obelisks and is completely detached from the septum checker's pit annulus. A nylon bag is introduced into the thoracic cavity using a trial M.M. Choker for tumor containment.
The surgeon meticulously resects the tumor from its attachment to the interatrium septum, ensuring complete removal, and places it into a nylon bag for containment.

The surgeon inspects the head chambers using a three-dimensional angloscope to confirm complete tumor removal, ensuring no residual tissue remains. The right atrium is then closed with a double-layer running suture using zero-poly thread.

A 13-French drain is placed in the pericardial cavity. The tumor-containing nylon bag is pulled out through a 12-mm trocar hole after reducing the tumor volume. A 32-French drain is inserted into the pericardial cavity through a 10-mm trocar position.
